forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Partly cloudy, with a low around 11. Southwest wind 6 to 10 mph.

forecast.name: Wednesday
forecast.shortForecast: Light Snow then Chance Light Rain
forecast.detailedForecast: Snow between 7am and noon, then a chance of rain between noon and 2pm. Cloudy, with a high near 40. Southwest wind 8 to 15 mph, with gusts as high as 29 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New snow accumulation of 1 to 2 inches possible.

weather_report:
As twilight envelops Boston, the city lies under a patchwork of shimmering lights, each flicker a subtle reminder of life bustling beneath the soft, starless sky. The evening air is crisp, with whispers of chill tracing pathways along the skin. Tonight promises to be partly cloudy, with temperatures dipping to a cool 11 degrees. A gentle southwest wind ushers in the evening, a quiet herald of what tomorrow may hold.

By dawn, a transformative snowfall will blanket the city, anticipated to arrive in lullabies of light white flurries between the hours of morning and noon. The prospect of watching the familiar elements of the city slip under a layer of snow feels both magical and serene, even if it entails the chilly embrace of a high barely reaching 40 degrees amid sporadic rainfall. The day ahead will be marked by gusts, raucous yet invigorating, urging one to wrap up in warm layers. To embrace the day, consider thick, protective garments; perhaps a sturdy pair of boots and a cozy scarf would elevate the experience, ensuring warmth and comfort while savoring this fleeting winter’s embrace. The allure of a warm coffee shop beckons—perhaps a retreat to a treasured corner café, where the snow can be admired from the window, a cozy cocoon from which to witness the unfolding of this picturesque wintry scene.
